finding_results.notes: The cistern exchange now makes Bett the clear parental figure for Nori, names Hala as Bett's sister/aunt, and later references keep Nori aligned to Bett without reintroducing a competing maternal map.

The opening now keeps one paragraph on the departure image, one on Jin's lie to Melie, and one on the concrete social humiliation of being left before pivoting back to Melie's laughter in the present.
The repeated swallowed-stone shame metaphor has been replaced with Jin-specific social self-consciousness and bodily vigilance around how other people inspect his domestic life.
